question: The scatter plot shows CO₂ emissions vs. temperature anomaly. Which statement best describes the relationship shown by the plotted data?

options.A: Higher CO₂ emissions are generally associated with higher temperature anomalies.
options.B: Higher CO₂ emissions are generally associated with lower temperature anomalies.
options.C: The points form two completely separate clusters with no overlap in emissions.
options.D: There is no noticeable relationship between CO₂ emissions and temperature anomaly.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the y-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
